Politics.headline: Trump Threatens to Obliterate Iran's Energy Facilities If Deal Not Reached
Politics.detail: US President Donald Trump has warned that he will obliterate Iranian energy infrastructure if a deal to end the conflict is not reached soon and if the Strait of Hormuz trade is not immediately reopened. He added that attacks on infrastructure would be in retaliation for nearly 50 years of Iranian attacks on US soldiers.

Politics.headline: Spain Closes Airspace to US Military Aircraft Involved in Iran War
Politics.detail: Spain's defense ministry announced that Madrid had closed its airspace to all US military aircraft involved in the Iran war, and Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has called the US and Israeli attacks on Iran reckless and illegal. The closure forces US military aircraft to reroute around the NATO member state on missions to the Middle East.

Politics.headline: Israel Passes Law to Give Death Penalty to Palestinians Convicted of Lethal Attacks
Politics.detail: Israel's parliament passed a law making the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks. This move has drawn international condemnation.

Politics.headline: King Charles's State Visit to US to Go Ahead Despite Iran War Concerns
Politics.detail: King Charles's state visit to the US will take place in April despite calls to delay amid the Iran war. The visit is expected to focus on strengthening ties between the UK and the US.

Politics.headline: Putin to hold talks with Armenia PM Pashinyan on April 1
Politics.detail: Vladimir Putin will hold talks with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on April 1 to discuss the strategic partnership between Russia and Armenia, cooperation within the Eurasian space, and development of economic and transport ties in the South Caucasus. Pashinyan will be in Russia on a working visit.

Finance & Economy.headline: The price of gas is at a three-year high
Finance & Economy.detail: Gas prices have reached a three-year high, impacting consumers and businesses alike.

Finance & Economy.headline: Too much AI in the office is causing "brain fry"
Finance & Economy.detail: A new study indicates that overuse of AI agents in the workplace may lead to cognitive overload, or "brain fry."

Finance & Economy.headline: Developing countries are being priced out, in struggle for affordable finance
Finance & Economy.detail: The UN reports that developing countries are facing increasing difficulties in accessing affordable finance needed for sustainable development, due to sovereign credit ratings often overstating risk.

Finance & Economy.headline: Eurozone inflation hits highest since January 2025
Finance & Economy.detail: The annual inflation rate in the Eurozone increased to 2.5% in March, which is the highest it's been since January 2025, with energy prices rising due to the war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Small businesses cautious on hiring amid economic uncertainty
Finance & Economy.detail: Small businesses are exhibiting caution in their hiring practices due to prevailing economic uncertainties.

Technology.headline: FCC's Router Ban Quietly Places an Expiration Date on Home Internet Security
Technology.detail: The FCC ban on foreign-made routers may inadvertently create an expiration date for home internet security, requiring users to update hardware sooner than expected. This could lead to increased e-waste and costs for consumers.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: Microsoft is alerting users to the growing threat of cybercriminals using AI to enhance their attacks, automating phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance. This is accelerating the speed and sophistication of cyberattacks.

Technology.headline: Rec Room shutting down: Once valued at $3.5B, social gaming platform finds profits elusive
Technology.detail: Rec Room, a Seattle-based social gaming platform once valued at $3.5 billion, is shutting down on June 1 after a decade-long run. The company has struggled to achieve profitability.

Technology.headline: Amazon's Big Spring Sale Ends Tomorrow: Get Up to 50% Off on Smartphones by Google, Samsung, and More
Technology.detail: Amazon's Big Spring Sale is ending tomorrow, offering discounts of up to 50% on smartphones from brands like Google and Samsung. This is the last chance to grab these deals.

Technology.headline: OnePlus Nord 6 to feature massive 9,000mAh battery and Snapdragon 8s Gen 4
Technology.detail: OnePlus is releasing the Nord 6, which includes a 9,000mAh battery and Snapdragon 8s Gen 4. It is expected to be released in India on April 7.

Science.headline: Viagra Could Help Treat Devastating Childhood Disease
Science.detail: A surprising breakthrough suggests that a drug best known as Viagra could help treat a devastating childhood disease; researchers found that sildenafil significantly improved symptoms in patients.

Science.headline: Shape-shifting material mimics octopus camouflage
Science.detail: A new shape-shifting material can change both its texture and color in seconds, inspired by the camouflage abilities of octopuses by precisely controlling how a polymer swells with water.

Science.headline: Scientists Create 'Phonon Laser'
Science.detail: Scientists have taken lasers beyond light and into the realm of sound, creating a breakthrough “phonon laser” that manipulates tiny vibrations at the quantum level and reduces noise.

Science.headline: Quantum physics can confirm where someone is located
Science.detail: A recent post in Physics discusses how quantum physics can be used to confirm someone's location.

Science.headline: NASA begins the countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years
Science.detail: NASA has begun the countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years.

Health.headline: Calgary for-profit plasma clinic rated 'non-compliant' during Health Canada inspection
Health.detail: A for-profit plasma clinic in Calgary received a 'non-compliant' rating during a Health Canada inspection. The inspection raises concerns about the clinic's adherence to health and safety standards.

Health.headline: More than 67,500 Ontario kids waiting for core autism funding as demand grows
Health.detail: The demand for autism funding in Ontario is growing, with over 67,500 children waiting for core services. This backlog highlights the challenges in providing timely support for families and children with autism.

Health.headline: Simple Therapies Beat Drugs for Knee Arthritis Pain Relief
Health.detail: A major analysis showed that non-drug treatments like knee braces, hydrotherapy, and exercise can significantly ease knee osteoarthritis symptoms, reducing pain and improving mobility. These approaches offer alternatives to medication for managing knee arthritis.

Health.headline: Powerful Cholesterol Drug Cuts Heart Attack Risk by 31%
Health.detail: Researchers found that evolocumab, a cholesterol-lowering drug typically used for people who already have cardiovascular disease, can significantly cut the risk of first-time heart attack. The study suggests this drug could change heart disease prevention strategies.

Health.headline: More women are having babies later in life. How to improve your chance of a healthy pregnancy.
Health.detail: A federal report showed that 21% of all births in the U.S. were to women 35 and older in 2023, up from 8.8% in 1990. The article discusses ways to improve the chance of a healthy pregnancy when having babies later in life.

Sports.headline: Edwards, Makeer power South Carolina past TCU 78-52 to reach the Final Four
Sports.detail: South Carolina dominated TCU with a score of 78-52, securing their spot in the Final Four, powered by outstanding performances from Edwards and Makeer.

Sports.headline: Sheinbaum meets FIFA's Infantino to review Mexico's 2026 World Cup preparations
Sports.detail: Sheinbaum and Infantino met to discuss and assess Mexico's arrangements for hosting the 2026 World Cup.

Sports.headline: NFL taps Seahawks to be featured on HBO's “Hard Knocks” for 1st time in team history, AP source says
Sports.detail: According to an AP source, the Seattle Seahawks will be featured on HBO's “Hard Knocks” for the first time in the team's history.

Sports.headline: Toronto Maple Leafs fire general manager Brad Treliving
Sports.detail: The Toronto Maple Leafs have announced the dismissal of their general manager, Brad Treliving.

Sports.headline: Cal Raleigh, the 2025 AL MVP runner-up, walks it off for Mariners in 2-1 win against Yankees
Sports.detail: Cal Raleigh, who was the AL MVP runner-up in 2025, secured a 2-1 victory for the Mariners against the Yankees with a walk-off play.

Entertainment.headline: Belle Mariano appeals to fandoms to stop pitting people against each other
Entertainment.detail: Filipina actress Belle Mariano has asked her fans to stop creating conflict and comparisons between individuals.

Entertainment.headline: Holy Week 2026: Tagalog-dubbed 'Conclave,' more films set for TV broadcast
Entertainment.detail: Several films, including a Tagalog-dubbed version of 'Conclave,' are scheduled for television broadcast during Holy Week 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Aiko Melendez's daughter Marthena makes acting debut in short film
Entertainment.detail: Marthena, the daughter of actress Aiko Melendez, is making her debut as an actress in a short film.

Entertainment.headline: OTT releases of the week [April 1-5, 2026]: Maamla Legal Hai 2, Sitaare Zameen Par, Vadh 2 and others
Entertainment.detail: Several new films and web series are scheduled for release on OTT platforms between April 1st and 5th, including 'Maamla Legal Hai 2,' 'Sitaare Zameen Par,' and 'Vadh 2'.

Entertainment.headline: Singapore resort said to halt controversial dolphin sourcing, breeding
Entertainment.detail: Resorts World Sentosa in Singapore has reportedly stopped sourcing dolphins from the wild and suspended its captive breeding program, and is assembling experts to decide the future of its Indo-Pacific bottlenose dolphins.

Crime & Justice.headline: Israel passes death penalty law for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
Crime & Justice.detail: Israel's parliament has approved a law imposing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted in military courts of deadly attacks, a move criticized internationally as discriminatory. The law, applying only to those whose attacks aim to end Israel's existence, mandates execution by hanging within 90 days of sentencing, though life imprisonment remains an option in special circumstances.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cop killer Dezi Freeman shot dead after months on the run
Crime & Justice.detail: Dezi Freeman, who killed two Australian police officers last year and had been on the run for over seven months, has been fatally shot by police. The shooting occurred at a rural address in northeast Victoria during an operation to locate Freeman.

Crime & Justice.headline: Lawyers for Kirk suspect ask to delay hearing
Crime & Justice.detail: Defense attorneys for Tyler Robinson, accused of killing Charlie Kirk, are requesting a delay in the preliminary hearing, stating that an ATF analysis couldn't definitively link the bullet to the gun. The lawyers seek more time to review the evidence in light of the analysis.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man, woman dead in suspected murder-suicide in north Phoenix
Crime & Justice.detail: Phoenix police are investigating a suspected murder-suicide after a man and a woman were discovered deceased with gunshot wounds inside a residence near 39th Avenue and Salter Drive. The investigation is ongoing.

Crime & Justice.headline: NYC to roll out anti-street harassment ad campaign this week
Crime & Justice.detail: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's office is launching an ad campaign this week focused on domestic and gender-based violence. The campaign aims to address street harassment in the city.

Education.headline: Protests and cancellations mar Trump administration's America 250 tour
Education.detail: There is no evidence that the events themselves push a political message, but the coalition sponsoring the tour is composed entirely of conservative groups.

Education.headline: Schools in England must be compelled to offer pupils healthy food, not junk
Education.detail: The Guardian reports on the need to mandate healthy food options in English schools, moving away from less nutritious alternatives.

Education.headline: NEA Defends Birthright Protections from the Trump Administration
Education.detail: The National Education Association (NEA) is advocating for the protection of students' citizenship rights in public schools, which they say are currently under threat.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education to Downsize Footprint in Washington, D.C. and Save Taxpayers Over $4.8 Million Annually
Education.detail: Education Secretary Linda McMahon announced that the U.S. Department of Education will move out of the LBJ headquarters to save taxpayer money.

Education.headline: Yes, Grade Inflation Is Real. But Is It a Real Problem?
Education.detail: The National Education Association reports on the phenomenon of grade inflation, where grades have been rising for decades, and questions whether it's a genuine issue.

Lifestyle.headline: More women are having babies later in life. How to improve your chance of a healthy pregnancy.
Lifestyle.detail: As more women are choosing to have children later in life, experts share advice on how to improve the chances of a healthy pregnancy.

Lifestyle.headline: Worries about flying seem to be taking off. Here's how to cope with in-flight anxiety.
Lifestyle.detail: With increasing concerns about air travel, this article provides tips and strategies for managing anxiety during flights.

Lifestyle.headline: Colorectal cancer is rising in younger adults. Here's who is most at risk and symptoms to watch for.
Lifestyle.detail: This article discusses the increasing rates of colorectal cancer among younger adults, identifying risk factors and symptoms to be aware of.

Lifestyle.headline: Ways to use mint sauce without having to roast a lamb.
Lifestyle.detail: Explore creative culinary uses for mint sauce beyond the traditional pairing with roast lamb.

Lifestyle.headline: Sami Tamimi's recipes for slow-cooked lamb with spicy pickled lemon and jewelled Easter rice.
Lifestyle.detail: Chef Sami Tamimi shares recipes for a flavorful slow-cooked lamb dish accompanied by spicy pickled lemon and jewelled Easter rice.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Three cautionary tales from the Iran energy shock
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Germany, Taiwan and California are cautionary tales of governments that prioritized green movements, forcing premature retirement of nuclear reactors, coal plants and oil refineries, making them vulnerable in the face of the Iran war's impact on global energy markets. The error was confusing good intentions with good planning and assuming a stable global energy market would always be there.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Supreme Court's birthright citizenship options
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Supreme Court will hear arguments on President Trump's executive order to deny citizenship to children of immigrants, with the question of whether the justices will make a sweeping constitutional ruling or a narrower one that allows Congress to legislate on the subject. The administration's chances of winning are extremely low.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Demolishing blight makes space for growth
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The City of Pittsburgh has undertaken a major blitz of demolitions of blighted properties during this season which should mark a turning point for its relationship with blight, which is choking off hope and growth in neighborhoods that desperately need both. Targeted blight remediation through demolitions is one of the most efficient ways to use the money the city has.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Good mental health policy respects both civil liberties and treatment | Opinion
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Mental health policy debates are often framed as a stark choice between civil liberties and treatment, but in reality, the goal should be a system that respects both, ensuring Mainers with mental illness have access to high-quality services, due process protections, and a reliable and transparent system. A treatment framework should be evaluated not only by individual outcomes, but also by whether it operates safely, lawfully and consistently across the system.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: At Passover, I think of families like mine, forced to wander
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author reflects on her grandparents' refugee story, who fled to the Soviet Union in 1939 ahead of the Nazi arrival, and the decisions and outcomes they faced, drawing parallels to the biblical story of wandering.  The author connects this to the United States closing its doors to refugees and President Trump degrading those forced to flee.

World.headline: US Reopens Embassy in Venezuela in Significant Thawing of Relations
World.detail: The United States has reopened its embassy in Venezuela, signaling a significant step in the improving relationship between the two countries.

World.headline: Avi Lewis, Elected to Lead Canada's New Democratic Party, Promises 'NDP Comeback'
World.detail: Avi Lewis has been elected to lead Canada's New Democratic Party (NDP), vowing to revitalize the party and stage a comeback in Canadian politics.

World.headline: China to Ban Storing Remains of Dead in 'Bone Ash Apartments'
World.detail: China is set to implement a ban on storing human remains in structures known as 'bone ash apartments'.

World.headline: Kuwaiti Tanker Hit by Iranian Drone Attack in Dubai Port
World.detail: A Kuwaiti oil tanker was struck by a drone attack in Dubai's port, heightening tensions in the region amidst ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Israel Passes Law to Give Death Penalty to Palestinians Convicted of Lethal Attacks
World.detail: The Israeli government has passed a law that would impose the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of carrying out deadly attacks.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwean arrested with over US$11,000 tik in N1 truck bust in Western Cape
Zimbabwe.detail: A Zimbabwean was arrested in the Western Cape, South Africa, for possession of over US$11,000 worth of tik, a street name for methamphetamine, while driving a truck on the N1 highway.

Zimbabwe.headline: Citizens cry foul as chaos mars CAB3 hearings
Zimbabwe.detail: Hearings regarding the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) were marred by chaos, leading to citizen complaints.

Zimbabwe.headline: Businessman in US$368K money-laundering storm
Zimbabwe.detail: A businessman is facing allegations related to a US$368,000 money-laundering scheme.

Zimbabwe.headline: Sex workers must be protected by law: MP
Zimbabwe.detail: A Member of Parliament has advocated for legal protections for sex workers.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwean boxer in ICU after Mpumalanga bout
Zimbabwe.detail: A Zimbabwean boxer is in intensive care after a boxing match in Mpumalanga, South Africa.
